title: phaseolus candidus subf. intermedius
intro: Phaseolus candidus subf. intermedius, also known as the intermediate or white kidney bean, is a plant in the legume family that is native to Asia and is often used as a medicinal herb. This plant contains a high concentration of phytohemagglutinin (PHA), a compound with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus candidus subf. intermedius include reducing inflammation and improving blood sugar control, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
